subtitle.en: Kalyana Varma's Saravali (9th century) and Vaidyanatha's Jataka Parijata (14th century)  –  two essential hora texts for comparative reading and deepening chart interpretation
subtitle.hi: कल्याण वर्मा की सारावली (9वीं शताब्दी) और वैद्यनाथ की जातक पारिजात (14वीं शताब्दी)  –  तुलनात्मक अध्ययन और कुण्डली व्याख्या को गहरा करने के लिए दो आवश्यक होरा ग्रन्थ
subtitle.mai: कल्याण वर्मा की सारावली (9वीं शताब्दी) और वैद्यनाथ की जातक पारिजात (14वीं शताब्दी)  –  तुलनात्मक अध्ययन और कुण्डली व्याख्या को गहरा करने के लिए दो आवश्यक होरा ग्रन्थ
subtitle.mr: Kalyana Varma's Saravali (9th century) and Vaidyanatha's Jataka Parijata (14th century)  –  two essential hora texts for comparative reading and deepening chart interpretation

explanation.en: Kalyana Varma, a king from the Vyala dynasty of central India, wrote Saravali in the 9th century CE. The name Saravali means 'The Essence (Sara) Collection (Avali).' It is notable for its extremely detailed planet-in-sign descriptions  –  12 signs x 9 planets = 108 detailed descriptions that go far beyond what BPHS provides.
explanation.hi: कल्याण वर्मा, मध्य भारत के व्यल वंश के राजा, ने 9वीं शताब्दी में सारावली लिखी। नाम का अर्थ है 'सार (सार) संग्रह (आवली)।'
explanation.mr: Kalyana Varma, a king from the Vyala dynasty of central India, wrote Saravali in the 9th century CE. The name Saravali means 'The Essence (Sara) Collection (Avali).' It is notable for its extremely detailed planet-in-sign descriptions  –  12 signs x 9 planets = 108 detailed descriptions that go far beyond what BPHS provides.

question.en: What makes Saravali unique compared to other hora texts?
question.hi: सारावली को अन्य होरा ग्रन्थों से क्या अलग बनाता है?
question.mr: What makes Saravali unique compared to other hora texts?

explanation.en: Saravali's unique contribution is its exhaustive planet-in-sign descriptions  –  108 detailed readings (9 planets x 12 signs). While BPHS gives brief results and Phaladeepika focuses on planets in houses, Saravali provides multi-paragraph descriptions for each planet-sign combination including physical appearance, temperament, career tendencies, and life patterns.
explanation.hi: सारावली का अद्वितीय योगदान इसके विस्तृत ग्रह-राशि वर्णन हैं  –  108 विस्तृत फल (9 ग्रह x 12 राशि)।
explanation.mr: Saravali's unique contribution is its exhaustive planet-in-sign descriptions  –  108 detailed readings (9 planets x 12 signs). While BPHS gives brief results and Phaladeepika focuses on planets in houses, Saravali provides multi-paragraph descriptions for each planet-sign combination including physical appearance, temperament, career tendencies, and life patterns.

options.hi: यह केवल मुहूर्त पर ध्यान देती है
यह तमिल में लिखी गई थी
यह केवल स्त्री ज्योतिष कवर करती है

question.en: Who wrote Jataka Parijata and in which century?
question.hi: जातक पारिजात किसने और किस शताब्दी में लिखी?
question.mr: Who wrote Jataka Parijata and in which century?

explanation.en: Vaidyanatha Dikshita wrote Jataka Parijata in the 14th century CE. The title means 'The Coral Tree of Horoscopy' (Jataka = horoscope, Parijata = the celestial coral tree). It synthesizes multiple earlier traditions and is especially valued for its systematic treatment of yogas, planetary combinations, and longevity calculation.
explanation.hi: वैद्यनाथ दीक्षित ने 14वीं शताब्दी में जातक पारिजात लिखी। शीर्षक का अर्थ है 'ज्योतिष का पारिजात वृक्ष।'
explanation.mr: Vaidyanatha Dikshita wrote Jataka Parijata in the 14th century CE. The title means 'The Coral Tree of Horoscopy' (Jataka = horoscope, Parijata = the celestial coral tree). It synthesizes multiple earlier traditions and is especially valued for its systematic treatment of yogas, planetary combinations, and longevity calculation.
